postmessage_no_origin_check.description: WSTG-CLNT-11 -- window.addEventListener('message', handler) handler does not check event.origin, accepts data from any cross-origin frame. Attacker iframes the target and postMessage's payload -> XSS / state injection / auth bypass.

probes.description: JS body contains addEventListener('message', ...) without event.origin or e.origin check -- handler trusts any sender. Sink chain: handler reads event.data and assigns to innerHTML/eval/location.
postMessage handler with origin === '*' or wildcard match -- explicitly accepts any origin
Sender uses postMessage(payload, '*') -- broadcast to any embedding parent, leaks data through eavesdropping iframe

postmessage_data_into_sink.description: WSTG-CLNT-11 -- handler reads event.data and writes to a dangerous sink (innerHTML, document.write, eval, Function, location.href, setTimeout-string). Combine with no-origin-check for full XSS chain.

probes.description: Message handler assigns event.data (or destructured field) into innerHTML / outerHTML / document.write / eval
Message handler assigns event.data to location.href / location = / window.location -- open-redirect or XSS via javascript: URL

xssi_json_array_callable.description: WSTG-CLNT-13 -- legacy JSON array endpoint returns top-level array `[{user:1},...]` -- valid JavaScript when loaded via <script src>. Attacker on evil.com overrides Array constructor to read victim's data cross-origin.

probes.description: Authenticated JSON response starts with top-level array AND lacks Content-Type protection -- cross-origin script-tag include reads it

xssi_jsonp_callback_unfiltered.description: WSTG-CLNT-13 -- JSONP endpoint reflects callback name without alphanumeric filter -- attacker passes callback=alert(1); to inject arbitrary JS into any victim's page

probes.description: Reflected JSONP callback name accepts non-alphanumeric -- ( in callback is reflected unfiltered

xssi_secrets_in_script_includable.description: WSTG-CLNT-13 -- script-includable response (text/javascript or application/javascript content type) contains user-specific secrets accessible via global side effects (var/window assignment)

probes.description: JS-content-typed response with global var assignment of user-specific token/email/id -- attacker includes script cross-origin to harvest
